explicit_text: I served in the United States Army infantry for three years during World War II. My division was the 84th Infantry Division, and we fought primarily in the European Theater of Operations. The Army was my organization throughout the war, and I experienced many campaigns and battles under its command.

My division was the 84th Infantry Division, and we fought primarily in the European Theater of Operations. This military branch was my organization throughout the war, and I experienced many campaigns and battles under its command.

explicit_text: I was proud to serve in the 84th Infantry Division during World War II. We landed in Normandy in September and fought through France, Paris, Holland, and into Germany. The division took part in major campaigns like the Rhineland and Central Europe, and we were heavily involved in the Battle of the Bulge. I even got to reconnect with fellow veterans at reunions and the World War II Memorial.

explicit_text: The Battle of the Bulge was my most memorable wartime experience. We were pretty isolated for a long time because of the weather, and we couldn’t get any support. It was around Christmas time 1944, and after surviving that tough battle, getting relieved felt like a vacation. We stayed involved there until late January before moving back to the Rhineland.

explicit_text: I visited the World War II Memorial about two weeks ago and was very impressed by it. While I appreciated the overall design, I wished there were more personal touches related to specific battles like Iwo Jima that veterans could connect with. Still, it’s a beautiful tribute and a credit to those who built it, especially considering concerns about disrupting the view of the Mall.

explicit_text: On the way back to New York after the war in Europe ended, we traveled on a Puerto Rican banana boat across the Atlantic. It was a flat-bottom boat with no keel, and the storms made the trip very rough. I spent most of the fourteen-day journey in my bunk trying to get through the seasickness.

explicit_text: After the rough Atlantic crossing on the Puerto Rican banana boat, we finally arrived in New York City. It was a relief to be back home after years of fighting in Europe. New York was the port where we disembarked and began our transition back to civilian life.
